excerpt: Through our faithful servants, we have rendered falsehood equal to truth and made temporal authorities like the righteous indistinguishable from one another.
source_location_hint: Concerning the obscuration of truth through multiplicity of discourse

excerpt: Once that intellect (al-ʿAql) awakens, there is nothing we can accomplish.
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql (The Repose of the Intellect)
source_location_hint: On the role of intellect in spiritual ascent

excerpt: Each desires Paradise, yet none wishes to die—is it not remarkable that God Almighty should expect loyalty and obedience from such an unfaithful and foolish creature?
source_location_hint: On human contradictions and the desire for eternal life without accepting death

excerpt: What he perceives as burden, they consider blessing and divine grace, for they know they shall receive eternal peace in return for this temporary effort.
source_work: Taʾwil al-Daʿaʾim (Esoteric Exegesis of the Ordinances)
source_location_hint: On the hidden meanings of ascetic practices and their spiritual fruits

excerpt: There will be no imaginary companion, and no living one shall remain—mutual ingratitude keeps them distant.
source_work: Jamiʿ al-Hikmatayn (The Compilation of Two Wisdoms)
source_location_hint: On the erosion of conjugal bonds through forgetfulness of reciprocal kindnesses
